data.content:
# Implementation Plan — Create New: Groom then Take buttons (foolery-cc43)

## Goal
After a beat is created via the "Create New" dialog, surface two sequential
post-create actions: a **Groom** button that runs grooming on the new beat and
waits for completion, then a **Take** button that is enabled only after grooming
succeeds and starts a session (equivalent to the existing Take action).

## Key findings (reuse, don't reinvent)
- Create flow: `CreateBeatDialog` (src/components/create-beat-dialog.tsx,
  mounted from src/components/app-header.tsx:194) renders `BeatForm` in "create"
  mode. `useSubmitBeat` → `createBeat()` (src/lib/api.ts); on success it shows a
  toast and immediately closes via `onCreated()`. There is currently NO
  post-create action surface.
- Grooming = the existing stale-beat-grooming review pipeline. POST
  `/api/beats/stale-grooming/reviews` accepts arbitrary `{agentId?, targets:
  [{beatId, repoPath}]}` (NOT restricted to stale beats — canonicalizeTargets
  just resolves the beat), returns `{jobs:[{jobId,beatId,repoPath}], agentId}`.
  Worker runs async. Client helpers already exist in
  src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-api.ts: `enqueueStaleBeatGroomingReviews()` and
  `fetchStaleBeatGroomingReviews()` → `StaleBeatGroomingReviewRecord[]` with
  `jobId`, `status` (queued|running|completed|failed), `error`, `completedAt`.
  Completion = poll reviews, match our `jobId`, watch `status`.
- Take = `startSession(beatId, repo)` (src/lib/terminal-api.ts) + terminal-store
  `upsertTerminal()`. Core logic lives in `useShipBeat.handleShipBeat`
  (src/app/beats/use-ship-beat.ts). Eligibility guard = `canTakeBeat()`
  (src/lib/beat-take-eligibility.ts).
- The dialog lives in the app-header tree, separate from the beats page where
  `handleShipBeat` lives. Zustand stores (terminal-store, app-store) are global,
  so the dialog can drive Take directly without threading a page callback.

## Design
Turn the dialog into two phases. The form phase is unchanged; on the primary
("Done") submit success, instead of closing, enter a **post-create phase** that
renders the Groom→Take panel for the newly created beat. "Create More" keeps its
current rapid-create behavior (no panel). Closing the dialog (X / onOpenChange)
finalizes as today.

Sequence enforcement: Take is rendered `disabled` and its handler guards until
grooming status === "completed". Failed grooming surfaces the error and leaves
Take disabled — the sequence cannot be bypassed.

## Files to change

1. NEW `src/lib/grooming-status.ts` (pure, hermetically testable)
   - `deriveGroomingStatus(records: StaleBeatGroomingReviewRecord[], jobId):
     { status: "idle"|"queued"|"running"|"completed"|"failed"; error?: string }`.
   - Pure reducer over the polled records; no IO. This is the unit-test seam.

2. NEW `src/lib/use-beat-grooming.ts` — hook `useBeatGrooming(beatId, repo)`:
   - `startGroom()` → `enqueueStaleBeatGroomingReviews({ targets:[{beatId,
     repoPath: repo}] })`; store returned `jobId` in state. On `!ok`, toast the
     error (covers 503 unconfigured-agent) and stay idle.
   - react-query poll of `fetchStaleBeatGroomingReviews()` with `refetchInterval`
     ~2500ms, `enabled` only while a jobId is set and status not terminal.
   - Derive status via `deriveGroomingStatus`. On "failed", toast `error`.
   - Returns `{ status, error, startGroom, isGrooming }`.

3. NEW `src/components/create-beat-post-actions.tsx` — panel component:
   - Props: `beatId`, `title`, `repo`, `onDone()`.
   - Renders Groom button (label "Groom" → "Grooming…" with spinner while
     queued/running, disabled once started). Uses `useBeatGrooming`.
   - Renders Take button: disabled unless `status === "completed"`; title hint
     explains it unlocks after grooming. On click → `startBeatSession` helper
     (see #5), then `onDone()` to close. Guard re-checks status === completed.
   - Shows a short status line / failed-state message (error surfaced inline in
     addition to toast).
   - Keep under 500 lines / functions under 100 lines (extract subcomponents if
     needed).

4. MODIFY `src/components/create-beat-dialog.tsx`
   - Add state `createdBeat: { id; title; repo } | null` (+ reset on open=false).
   - In `handleSubmit` success: keep the success toast, call
     `invalidateBeatListQueries` (so the new beat lists even before Take), then
     set `createdBeat` instead of calling `onCreated()` immediately.
   - Render `<CreateBeatPostActions … onDone={onCreated}/>` when `createdBeat`
     set, else `<BeatForm/>`. "Create More" path unchanged.
   - `onCreated` is now invoked from the panel (after Take or via dialog close).

5. REFACTOR for Take parity (avoid duplicating upsert logic)
   - Extract the core of `handleShipBeat` into a shared helper
     `startBeatSession({ beatId, beatTitle, repo })` (new
     `src/lib/start-beat-session.ts` or a terminal-store action) that calls
     `startSession` + `upsertTerminal` and returns ok/err. Have BOTH
     `useShipBeat` and the post-actions panel use it. This keeps the Take
     "equivalent to the existing Take action" with no logic drift.
   - The fresh beat has no rolling ancestor / existing session, so those
     branches of `handleShipBeat` are not needed in the create path.

## Invariants / repo rules
- No new workflow-state string checks. Grooming status strings are the grooming
  job enum (not loom state classification); Take eligibility reuses
  `canTakeBeat`. Complies with "State Classification Is Loom-Derived".
- Grooming/Take failures surface loudly (toast + inline) — aligns with "Fail
  Loudly". No silent fallbacks; the 503 unconfigured-agent error is shown.
- Respect file/function/line length limits (500/100/100) — hence the split into
  helper + hook + panel rather than growing create-beat-dialog.tsx.

## Test strategy (hermetic)
- Unit: `src/lib/__tests__/grooming-status.test.ts` — `deriveGroomingStatus`
  across not-found→idle, queued, running, completed, failed (with error). Pure,
  no env.
- Unit: sequence guard — assert the panel's Take-enabled predicate is false for
  every non-"completed" status and true only for "completed".
- Storybook story for `create-beat-post-actions.tsx` with mocked api modules:
  idle, grooming (spinner, Take disabled), completed (Take enabled), failed
  (error shown, Take disabled). Run via `bun run test:all`.
- No network/fs/env in default suite; mock `stale-beat-grooming-api` and
  `terminal-api` at the module boundary.

## Risks / notes
- Grooming requires a configured stale-grooming agent; if absent the POST 503s.
  We surface that error and keep Take locked (acceptable; matches Fail-Loudly).
- Grooming on a brand-new beat may yield "abandon/merge" suggestions; out of
  scope — we only trigger + await, not auto-apply outcomes.
- Polling cost is bounded (panel only mounts for one beat, poll disabled once
  terminal). Reuses existing reviews store; no new server endpoint required.

## Complexity
Small–medium. ~2 new small files + 1 hook + 1 pure helper + targeted edits to
create-beat-dialog.tsx and a small extract from use-ship-beat.ts. No backend
route changes. No DB/migration work.
